items.title: Angus Taylor promises permanent tax cuts and to reinstate all negative gearing

children.content: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.

articles.title: 'Make a choice': Taylor defends comments aimed at non-citizens

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has defended his comments aimed at non-citizens as necessary and practical during a heated interview on 7.30.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie.

articles.title: Business groups lament migration plan as Taylor speaks

children.content: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but businesses say the economy needs a "sustainable" migration program.

image.altText: Close up of house under construction, frame and windows, no walls, no workers in shot.

gallery.altText: a male politician giving a speech in a chamber

thumbnail.altText: a male politician giving a speech in a chamber

items.title: Finally, a tax fight that actually means something

children.content: Labor and the Liberals are offering imperfect but undeniably bold alternative visions for the tax system. It's nice to have something to talk about.

image.altText: Angus Taylor and Anthony Albanese in a composite image. Both are in suits

items.title: Taylor's bold budget reply a mix of sound and dubious policy commitments

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor commits to phasing in income tax indexation under a "tax back guarantee" that he says "will fully protect all taxpayers from inflation".

items.title: Chinese Australians say Coalition pitch to cut non-citizens' welfare 'sounds like One Nation'

children.content: Chinese Australians have lashed the Coalition's plan to block non-citizens from accessing welfare services, likening the opposition's targeting of migrants to One Nation.

image.altText: Richard Shi wearing a black top and glasses stands in front of a building

items.title: Trust tax could hurt young, not just ultra-wealthy, tax advisors warn

children.content: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

image.altText: Seveal bank notes laying on dirt.

items.title: The food, music and praise served up at the Trump-Xi state banquet

children.content: Guests dined on Beijing roast duck and tiramisu while the People's Liberation Army band performed some classic hits.

articles.title: The key takeaways from Trump and Xi's meeting in China

children.content: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

articles.title: Who is in Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?

children.content: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

image.altText: Trump and Xi walk along a red carpet in front of rows of delegates standing on stone stairs.

items.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea after six years at London club

items.title: Leadership contenders edge towards challenging UK PM Starmer

children.content: The UK's health secretary has resigned from cabinet and another Labour MP has quit parliament paving the way for a potential leadership challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

image.altText: A composite image of two men.

items.title: The Liberals said John was dead, but he's considering a new political life

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: Ambulance data shows overdose callout at Crown Melbourne every two weeks

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, with the casino complex averaging an ambulance call-out for a suspected overdose every two weeks, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

image.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: How the wisdom of experience won the State of Origin series for New South Wales

children.content: New South Wales series win over Queensland proves that sometimes, like anything else, practice makes perfect in State of Origin football.

articles.title: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win

children.content: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line.

image.altText: Jasmine Peters is tackled into touch in Women's State of Origin II.

await you.

items.title: Abandoned base's toxic waste clean-up deadline rejected

children.content: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

items.title: 'Mega' gas plant planned to feed hungry AI-data centres

items.title: ICC fugitive flees Philippine Senate after shooting chaos

children.content: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court on charges of crimes against humanity, had been holed up inside the Philippine Senate since Monday to avoid arrest. His whereabouts is now unknown.

items.title: Email reveals Jewish group warned police about Bondi gunman in 2019

children.content: A Jewish security agency raised concerns about Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram years before the terror attack that left 15 people dead, a document shows.

items.title: Power to cut funding and stricter 'permanence' test under new NDIS

children.content: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

items.title: VAR drama, pitch invasion as Scottish league heads for final day shootout
